title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 6
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: You have been asked to write a report for the director of your college about the current state of the computer facilities. Mention the hardware, software, and the support available, and recommend improvements.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on College Computer Facilities

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to evaluate the current state of the computer facilities at the college and to suggest necessary upgrades. The findings are based on a survey of students across different departments.

Hardware and Software
The primary issue identified by students is the age of the hardware. The desktop computers in the main lab are over five years old, resulting in slow boot-up times and frequent system crashes. Regarding software, while the standard office suites are up to date, students in the design and engineering departments reported that specialized software is either missing or incompatible with the current operating systems. This significantly hinders their ability to complete assignments on campus.

Technical Support
On a positive note, the technical support team was highly praised. Staff are knowledgeable and resolve most software issues promptly. However, the support desk is only open during standard office hours, which does not assist those who use the facilities during evenings or weekends.

Recommendations
To ensure the facilities meet the needs of a modern student body, I recommend:
- A phased replacement of all desktop computers over the next 12 months.
- Investing in cloud-based software licenses to allow students to work from their own laptops while on campus.
- Implementing an online 'help ticket' system for after-hours technical issues.

Conclusion
Upgrading the hardware and software is essential for the college to remain competitive. Providing students with reliable tools is a prerequisite for academic success.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt: You recently went on a group activity holiday that was not what you expected. Write a letter to the holiday company, explaining what the problems were and how they affected your holiday.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to express my extreme disappointment with the 'Adrenaline Adventure' holiday I took in the Alps from February 5th to 12th. I chose this package specifically for its promise of a challenging and varied itinerary, but the reality was far from what was advertised.

Firstly, the 'expert-led' activities were poorly managed. On two occasions, our mountain biking guide appeared unfamiliar with the trails, leading us into a restricted area where we were stopped by local authorities. This was not only embarrassing but also dangerous. Furthermore, the equipment provided was in poor condition; my safety helmet had a noticeable crack, and several bikes had faulty gear systems.

Secondly, the accommodation was significantly below the promised standard. The brochure described a 'luxury mountain lodge', but we were placed in a basic hostel with no private facilities and inadequate heating. This made it impossible to rest properly after a day of physical exertion, which was exhausting and demoralising for the whole group.

These failures meant that instead of a relaxing and exciting holiday, I spent most of my time dealing with logistical errors and physical discomfort. I believe that your company has failed to deliver the service described in the contract. Therefore, I am requesting a refund of 50% of the total cost as compensation for the substandard experience.

I look forward to your prompt response.

Yours faithfully,
David Miller

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen this post on an international student website: 'We want reviews of museums or art galleries you have visited recently. Tell us what you liked, what you didn't like, and whether it's worth a visit for other students.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
The Museum of Modern Minds: A Mixed Experience

Last week, I visited the 'Museum of Modern Minds', a new interactive gallery in the city centre. While the concept is ambitious, the execution left me with mixed feelings.

What I liked most was the immersive nature of the exhibits. Unlike traditional museums, here you are encouraged to touch and interact with the art. One highlight was the 'Digital Mirror' room, which uses motion sensors to transform your movements into abstract light patterns. It’s visually stunning and incredibly fun for a group of friends. The use of technology to explain complex psychological concepts is truly innovative.

However, there were several drawbacks. The museum is clearly designed for 'Instagram moments', which means it gets incredibly crowded with people taking selfies, making it difficult to actually read the educational plaques or reflect on the art. Additionally, the entry fee is quite steep at €18, and there is no student discount available, which seems like a missed opportunity for a venue aimed at a younger audience.

Is it worth a visit? For a student on a budget, perhaps not. While it’s a great place for a couple of cool photos, it lacks the depth of more established galleries. If you're looking for a serious art experience, you might be disappointed. However, if you have some cash to spare and want a light-hearted, tech-filled afternoon, it’s a fun way to spend a couple of hours. Just don't expect to leave with a deep understanding of modern philosophy.
